steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to introduce Elizabeth Catlett to an audience that does not know her. An effective introduction must identify who she is and what she is known for — not assume prior familiarity and jump straight to isolated details about specific works.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In contrast to the 1971 sculpture Political Prisoner, which is made of cedar, the 1980 sculpture Singing Head is made of marble.: This sentence names two works and their materials but never says who Catlett is or what she is known for. An unfamiliar reader learns nothing about the artist herself. ✗
  • Choice B — Political Prisoner is a cedar sculpture that was created in 1971.: This describes a single sculpture without naming Catlett at all. It does not introduce the artist. ✗
  • Choice C — The sculpture Political Prisoner was made in 1971, and the sculpture Singing Head was made in 1980.: This lists two works with their dates but again omits who Catlett is and what she is celebrated for. ✗
  • Choice D — Elizabeth Catlett, a celebrated African American artist, is best known for creating sculptures and prints that explore the Black experience.: This names Catlett, identifies her as a celebrated African American artist, and states what she is best known for — providing exactly the context an unfamiliar audience needs. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
